summary: Review Application Insights components and instrumented app settings for visible sampling, filtering, and logging posture clues.

targets.disruption_reason: filtering and sampling posture clues are both visible; current identity has visible app configuration write control

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Changes app settings that can control Application Insights instrumentation behavior.
capability_steps.boundary: Does not read code-level instrumentation bodies.

capability_steps.api_surface: Application Insights component and app settings
capability_steps.downstream_effect: Selects the instrumented app or function where telemetry is shaped.
capability_steps.boundary: A visible setting name is a posture clue, not proof of emitted telemetry.

capability_steps.api_surface: sampling app settings or SDK/OpenTelemetry config clues
capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can reduce retained request, dependency, trace, or exception examples while dashboards remain alive.
capability_steps.boundary: Does not prove true unsampled event count.

capability_steps.api_surface: filter, processor, or logging-level setting clues
capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can narrow selected telemetry types before investigators query Application Insights.
capability_steps.boundary: Does not prove filtered events occurred.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: The instrumentation posture remains as normal application configuration until changed.
capability_steps.boundary: Change timing and author require history.

capability_steps.api_surface: app setting names and component posture
capability_steps.downstream_effect: Common cover stories include cost control, health-check filtering, privacy, and performance tuning.
capability_steps.boundary: Cover story is not an intent claim.

current_identity_context.name: azurefox-lab-sp
current_identity_context.summary: Current foothold `azurefox-lab-sp` has visible app configuration write control.

current_state.posture: filtering clue(s) visible; sampling clue(s) visible; logging-level clue(s) visible

not_collected_by_default.name: setting values
not_collected_by_default.reason: Default output uses setting names as posture clues and does not print instrumentation keys or connection strings.

not_collected_by_default.name: code-level processors
not_collected_by_default.reason: Telemetry processor bodies usually live in source code or binaries, outside management-plane posture.

not_collected_by_default.name: host.json body
not_collected_by_default.reason: Function sampling can live in host.json; this helper only uses visible app setting names by default.

not_collected_by_default.name: true unsampled count
not_collected_by_default.reason: Current posture cannot prove how many events were dropped or retained.

not_collected_by_default.name: detector failure
not_collected_by_default.reason: The command does not inspect detections, so it cannot claim a rule missed activity.
